metadata.description: Deep security challenges validating path traversal prevention, XSS prevention in HTML output, query injection prevention, CancellationException rethrow safety, credential storage, HTTPS enforcement, certificate validation, and input sanitization across all protocol services and format parsers

challenges.name: Path Traversal Prevention via normalizePath()
challenges.description: Verify that normalizePath() in Dropbox, FTP, SFTP, and SMB services resolves '..' segments and enforces root boundary. Test with payloads including '../../../etc/passwd', '..\..\windows\system32', URL-encoded '%2e%2e%2f', double-encoded '%252e%252e%252f', and null-byte injection 'file%00.txt'. All must be neutralized to safe paths within the service root.

outputs.name: normalization_results
outputs.description: Map of protocol to list of payload/normalized-path pairs

outputs.name: traversal_escaped_root
outputs.description: Payloads that escaped the root boundary

assertions.message: No path traversal payload may escape the root boundary
At least 4 protocol services must be tested (Dropbox, FTP, SFTP, SMB)
All normalization results must show safe resolved paths

challenges.name: Symlink-Based Path Traversal Prevention
challenges.description: Verify that protocol services do not follow symbolic links that point outside the service root. Create test symlinks pointing to parent directories and verify that file operations through them are rejected or resolved safely.

outputs.name: symlinks_followed_unsafely
outputs.description: Symlinks that were followed to locations outside root

assertions.message: No symlink may be followed to a location outside the service root

challenges.name: XSS Prevention in HTML Output for All 17 Formats
challenges.description: Verify that all 17 format parsers properly escape or sanitize HTML special characters in their toHtml() output. Inject XSS payloads including '', '', 'javascript:alert(1)', '

outputs.name: xss_results
outputs.description: Map of format ID to list of XSS test results

outputs.name: vulnerable_formats
outputs.description: Formats where XSS payload appeared unescaped in HTML

assertions.message: No format parser may produce HTML containing unescaped XSS payloads
All 17 format parsers must be tested for XSS
All XSS test results must show proper escaping

challenges.name: CSS Injection Prevention in StyleSheets
challenges.description: Verify that StyleSheets.kt CSS generation does not allow CSS injection through format-controlled content or theme parameters. Test with payloads including 'expression(alert(1))', 'url(javascript:alert(1))', '-moz-binding', '@import url(evil.css)', and 'behavior:url(evil.htc)'. None must appear in generated CSS output.

assertions.message: No CSS injection payload may appear in generated stylesheets
All CSS injection tests must pass

challenges.name: Query Injection Prevention in Google Drive API
challenges.description: Verify that GoogleDriveService sanitizes API query strings by escaping single quotes, URL encoding, and JSON escaping. Test with payloads including SQL-like injection ('; DROP TABLE--'), single-quote escaping ('file' OR '1'='1'), and JSON injection ('{"key": "value"}') in file search and list operations.

outputs.name: sanitization_results
outputs.description: Results of query sanitization for each payload

outputs.name: unsanitized_payloads
outputs.description: Payloads that were not properly sanitized

assertions.message: All query payloads must be properly sanitized for Google Drive API
All sanitization tests must pass

challenges.name: Query Injection Prevention in OneDrive API
challenges.description: Verify that OneDriveService sanitizes OData filter expressions and search queries. Test with OData injection ('$filter=true eq true'), path injection ('/me/drive/root:/etc/passwd'), and Unicode normalization attacks in file paths.

assertions.message: All OData payloads must be sanitized for OneDrive API
All sanitization tests must pass

challenges.name: Query Injection Prevention in Dropbox API
challenges.description: Verify that DropboxService sanitizes JSON request bodies and path parameters. Test with JSON injection in file paths, path parameter pollution, and content-type confusion attacks.

assertions.message: All payloads must be sanitized for Dropbox API
All Dropbox sanitization tests must pass

challenges.name: CancellationException Rethrow in All Protocol Services
challenges.description: Verify that all catch blocks in all 8 protocol services (Dropbox, GoogleDrive, OneDrive, FTP, SFTP, SMB, WebDAV, S3) rethrow kotlin.coroutines.cancellation.CancellationException instead of swallowing it. Simulate coroutine cancellation during network operations and verify the exception propagates to the caller.

outputs.name: catch_blocks_verified
outputs.description: Total catch blocks verified across all services

assertions.message: No protocol service may swallow CancellationException
At least 40 catch blocks must be verified across all services
All services must correctly rethrow CancellationException

challenges.name: Credential Storage Plaintext Prevention
challenges.description: Scan the entire codebase for plaintext credentials, API keys, tokens, and secrets. Verify that AuthTokenManager uses SecureStorage from Security-KMP rather than plaintext SharedPreferences or file storage. Check that no hardcoded credentials exist in source files, test files, or configuration.

outputs.name: hardcoded_secrets
outputs.description: Hardcoded secrets found in source code

assertions.message: No plaintext credentials may exist in the codebase
AuthTokenManager must use SecureStorage from Security-KMP
No hardcoded secrets allowed in source code

challenges.name: Credential Leak Prevention in Logs
challenges.description: Verify that OAuth tokens, refresh tokens, API keys, and passwords are never written to application logs, crash reports, or debug output. Trigger authentication flows and network operations while capturing log output, then scan for leaked credentials.

outputs.name: redaction_working
outputs.description: Whether credential redaction is active in logging

assertions.message: No credentials may appear in log output
Credential redaction must be active in the logging system

challenges.name: HTTPS Enforcement for Cloud Protocol Services
challenges.description: Verify that Dropbox, Google Drive, and OneDrive services enforce HTTPS for all API calls and reject HTTP. Check that base URLs use https://, that HTTP redirects are not followed to plain HTTP, and that mixed-content requests are blocked.

outputs.name: base_urls_secure
outputs.description: Whether all base URLs use https://

assertions.message: No cloud protocol may allow plain HTTP connections
All base URLs must use https://
All HTTPS enforcement checks must pass

challenges.name: TLS Certificate Validation
challenges.description: Verify that Ktor HTTP clients used by cloud protocol services perform proper TLS certificate validation. Check that self-signed certificates are rejected, expired certificates are rejected, hostname mismatches are detected, and certificate pinning is enforced where configured.

assertions.message: No invalid certificate may be accepted by protocol services
All certificate validation scenarios must pass

challenges.name: Input Sanitization for Filenames
challenges.description: Verify that filenames provided to protocol services are sanitized to remove dangerous characters including null bytes, control characters, path separators, shell metacharacters (;, |, &, $, `), and overly long names. Test across all 8 protocol services.

outputs.name: dangerous_filenames_accepted
outputs.description: Dangerous filenames that were accepted without sanitization

assertions.message: No dangerous filename may be accepted without sanitization
All 8 protocol services must be tested
All filename sanitization results must show safe outputs

challenges.name: Input Sanitization for Document Content
challenges.description: Verify that document content containing control characters (except newline, tab, carriage return), BOM markers, right-to-left override characters (U+202E), and zero-width characters is handled safely by all 17 format parsers without producing malicious HTML output.

outputs.name: dangerous_output_produced
outputs.description: Formats that produced dangerous HTML from malicious content

assertions.message: No format may produce dangerous HTML from malicious content
All 17 format parsers must be tested
All content sanitization results must be safe

challenges.name: CoroutineScope Lifecycle Safety
challenges.description: Verify that serviceScope in Dropbox, GoogleDrive, and OneDrive services is cancelled on reconnect and disconnect to prevent coroutine leaks. Simulate rapid connect/disconnect/reconnect cycles and verify no orphaned coroutines remain after disconnection.

outputs.name: scope_cancelled_on_disconnect
outputs.description: Whether serviceScope is properly cancelled on disconnect

assertions.message: No orphaned coroutines may remain after disconnect
serviceScope must be cancelled on disconnect for all cloud protocols
All lifecycle checks must pass

challenges.name: Memory Disclosure Prevention
challenges.description: Verify that sensitive data (tokens, passwords, decrypted content) is cleared from memory after use and does not appear in heap dumps or toString() representations. Check that AuthTokenManager clears token buffers and that protocol service error messages do not leak sensitive data.

outputs.name: sensitive_in_tostring
outputs.description: Sensitive data found in toString() output

outputs.name: sensitive_in_errors
outputs.description: Sensitive data found in error messages

assertions.message: No sensitive data may appear in toString() output
No sensitive data may appear in error messages
All memory disclosure tests must pass
